note: Canonical-assumptions ledger — one entry per cross-page industry number that multiple pages/themes cite. Purpose: every page should reference ONE canonical framing per metric; when a new source disagrees, add a variant here (never silently replace). build_assumptions.py renders _meta/assumptions.md + _dashboards/assumptions.html and scans pages for citations of each metric not yet reconciled into the ledger. Edit variants by hand; keep scope labels precise (units vs dollars, IT-load vs facility GW).

asic-vs-gpu-share.title: Custom-ASIC vs merchant-GPU share of AI accelerators
asic-vs-gpu-share.definition: ASIC/XPU vs merchant-GPU split of AI accelerator shipments — ALWAYS state basis (units vs dollars) and horizon.
asic-vs-gpu-share.canonical: Near-term unit share inflects to ASICs (JPM: 42% 2026E -> 53% 2027E, units) while long-term dollar/unit share settles ~65-70% GPU (BofA AI 2030, UBS durable 70/30). These are horizons, not contradictions — the debate is whether the 2027 unit inflection sticks or plateaus.
asic-vs-gpu-share.scope_note: Unit share ≠ dollar share (ASIC ASPs are far lower). NVDA-page '~70% defensible' framing = long-horizon accelerator share, consistent with BofA 65-70% by 2030, NOT a rebuttal of JPM's 2027 unit-share call.

variants.value: 42% ASIC / 58% GPU units 2026E -> 53% / 47% 2027E; ASIC units +109% y/y vs GPU +39% (2026)
~65-70% GPU / 30-35% ASIC by 2030; NVDA ~68-77% of accelerators CY25->CY30, AVGO 8%->16%

asic-vs-gpu-share.debate: Does the 2027 ASIC unit-share inflection (JPM) stick, or does GPU hold ~70% long-term (UBS/BofA)? Watch TPU v9 volumes, Trainium 3 ramp, MTIA.

wfe-tam.title: WFE market size CY26 / CY27 / CY28
wfe-tam.definition: Total wafer-fab equipment market ($bn) by calendar year.
wfe-tam.canonical: CY26 ~$140-160B (all sources agree). CY27: house $180-215B vs MS ~$191B vs UBS $200B. CY28 is the genuine conflict: house top end $260B vs UBS $250B vs MS-implied ~$230-240B; skeptic ceiling 'never $300B' (ex-Samsung foundry EVP). Swing variable = memory-capex intensity + EUV availability post-2027 + first NAND greenfield (CY28).
wfe-tam.scope_note: House CY28 top-of-range ($260B) sits ABOVE street — this is a deliberate house-vs-street edge, tracked, not an error.

variants.value: house CY28 $260B implies ASML ~110-120 EUV shipments 2028 (vs supply chain sized ~90; JPM: 110+ possible without new buildings)

wfe-tam.debate: CY28: house $260B top vs street $230-250B. Resolves on memory greenfield timing + EUV shipments. UNIT BRIDGE (2026-07-01): believing house $260B = believing ASML flexes to ~110-120 EUV ships in 2028 (above the ~90 supply-chain sizing; JPM says 110+ feasible). Base bottom-up demand supports ~88-95; the gap = faster DRAM node migration + 2029 pre-provisioning.

hyperscaler-capex.title: Hyperscaler / AI-datacenter capex CY25 / CY26 / CY27
hyperscaler-capex.definition: Aggregate capex of the big US hyperscalers — ALWAYS state the basket (top-4 = MSFT/AMZN/GOOGL/META; five-name adds ORCL).
hyperscaler-capex.canonical: Top-4: ~$410B 2025A -> ~$700-725B 2026E (+~75%) -> ~$1.1T 2027E (JPM AI Capex 2.0). Five-name consensus: ~$700B 2026 / ~$900B+ 2027. Buy-side 2027 bogey ~$1.2T ('could be 1.3-1.4', Jefferies). Constraint is power/physical buildout, not demand.
hyperscaler-capex.scope_note: Top-4 vs five-name basket differences masquerade as revisions. GOOG-only: ~$350B 2027 / ~$375B 2028 (MS Nowak 2026-06-29).
hyperscaler-capex.debate: Level agreed near-term; the debate is 2027+ durability (see macro-cycle: bubble vs supercycle).

ai-dc-power-gw.title: AI datacenter power (GW) — facility GW vs IT-load GW vs 800V-debate GW
ai-dc-power-gw.definition: THREE DISTINCT BASES — never mix: (1) facility GW deployed/added (grid + on-site); (2) IT-load GW (compute load in racks); (3) 800V-transition GW (subset of IT load moving to 800VDC).
ai-dc-power-gw.canonical: Facility: ~14-16 GW built 2026 / ~20 GW 2027 vs ~40-45 GW proposed (Jefferies satellite tracking, 50% haircut); ~138 GW capacity through 2030 (JPM). IT-load: ~300 GW by 2030 (+25% CAGR, JPM/Omdia) or ~240-280 GW global (JPM First Principles). 800V debate: Citi models 9 GW -> 21 GW 2025-30 — a SUBSET, not total deployment.
ai-dc-power-gw.scope_note: STANDING RULE: the 800V notes' GW figures (IT load ~300 GW, Citi 9->21 GW) must NOT be folded into total-GW-deployed answers. Citi's 9->21 GW is the AI-specific 800V-relevant increment.

variants.value: ~14-16 GW built 2026 / ~20 GW 2027 vs ~40-45 GW proposed (50% haircut)
variants.source: Jefferies / SinMax / Industrial Info satellite tracking

variants.value: ~9.4 GW US leased 1Q26 (5x y/y) / ~11 GW near-term pipeline
~21 GW built in prior 25 years vs ~40 GW of NVDA chips into US 2025-2030
~300 GW IT load by 2030 (+25% CAGR, >35 GW/yr new)

variants.source: JPM First Principles: AI Power Infrastructure

variants.scope: 800V-DEBATE GW — subset of IT load, keep separate

ai-dc-power-gw.debate: No numeric conflict — the risk is basis conflation. Any new GW datapoint must be labeled facility / IT-load / 800V-subset before it enters a page.

hbm-cycle.title: HBM — capacity, pricing, allocation, sold-out claims
hbm-cycle.definition: HBM wafer capacity, $/GB or $/Gb pricing (state which), allocation priority, sold-out horizon.
hbm-cycle.canonical: Sold out CY26 incl. HBM4 (Micron Q1 FY26). Capacity ~473k wspm end-26E -> ~668k end-27E (SemiAnalysis). Pricing: NVDA HBM ~$17-18/GB 2026 -> $30-32/GB 2027, +70-80% (Redburn); blended ASP +~30% (JPM/Micron). Live tension: general-server DRAM overtook HBM as allocation priority #1 (Redburn/TrendForce) yet HBM pricing still marches higher. 2027 supply revised 60B -> 50B Gb-equiv on allocation, not demand (Redburn).
hbm-cycle.scope_note: $/GB (stack) vs $/Gb (die) are ~8-16x apart — always state units. TAM frames: $35B'25 -> $100B'28 (Micron/JPM) vs $168B CY30 (BofA) are different horizons, both valid.

variants.value: sold out CY26 on price and volume, incl. HBM4
~123k -> ~331k -> ~473k -> ~668k wspm (end-23/25/26E/27E); HBM ~35% of DRAM wafer cap end-27E
blended HBM ASP +~30% next year (~10% like-for-like + mix)
general-server DRAM now allocation #1, HBM #2 (4-yr reversal); 2027 HBM supply 60B -> 50B Gb-equiv

hbm-cycle.debate: Allocation deprioritization (Redburn) vs pricing-marches-higher (JPM/TrendForce). Watch HBM4 LTA finalization (~90-100% up vs last year's LTA).

nvda-units-gw.title: NVDA GPU units / GW shipped per year
nvda-units-gw.definition: NVDA accelerator unit shipments and implied GW/yr — distinguish GPU units, Vera standalone CPU, Vera-Rubin racks.
nvda-units-gw.canonical: GPU units: 6.2M 2026 -> 8.9M 2027 -> 9.9M 2028 (JPM); Fubon '10M+ in 2027' consistent. Racks: 54.5k-62k NVL72 2027F (Nomura). Capstone GW frame: ~16 GW/yr sold 2026 -> ~25 GW 2027 (house, NVDA page 2026-06-17) — the bottoms-up cross-check vs JPM units.
nvda-units-gw.scope_note: Vera standalone CPU (500k 2026 -> 3M 2027 JPM; 5.75M units 2027 to ASE FoCoS per MS) is NOT the same series as Vera-Rubin racks (~2.0-2.2M units 2026, cut from 2.8-2.9M on HBM4 issues).

variants.source: JPM Jay / MS Asia AI Supply Chain

nvda-units-gw.debate: House 25 GW 2027 vs JPM 8.9M units — reconcile via W/unit assumptions when the Capstone GW model updates.

cowos-allocation.title: CoWoS capacity and NVDA allocation share
cowos-allocation.definition: NVDA's share of TSMC CoWoS capacity; TPU-substrate splits within Google.
cowos-allocation.canonical: NVDA ~50-55% of TSMC CoWoS 2027 (Fubon ~50%+; Nomura <55% vs NVDA's targeted ~60% — i.e. target likely missed on TPU squeeze). Within Google TPU: AVGO ~66-68% / MediaTek ~32-34% (Nomura).
cowos-allocation.scope_note: The 'targeted ~60%' is NVDA's ask, not an outcome — don't cite it as achieved share.
cowos-allocation.debate: Does NVDA claw back toward 60% or does TPU keep the squeeze on?

ai-tam-2030.title: AI TAM by 2030
ai-tam-2030.definition: AI datacenter TAM frames — ALWAYS state scope (systems-only vs total infra).
ai-tam-2030.canonical: BofA ~$1.7T CY30 AI-DC-systems TAM (accelerators ~$1.2T, networking ~$316B, optical ~$88B, HBM $168B). Jensen's '$3-4T end-of-decade AI infra' is a BROADER scope (adds power/grid/software) — not a conflict with BofA.
ai-tam-2030.scope_note: Never compare $1.7T (systems) against $3-4T (infra) as if same quantity.
ai-tam-2030.debate: Scope discipline only — systems vs infra.

dram-pricing-2026.title: Conventional DRAM pricing cadence Q3/Q4 2026
dram-pricing-2026.definition: QoQ contract-price growth for conventional DRAM, 2H26.
dram-pricing-2026.canonical: TrendForce REVISED (2026-06-30, supersedes 06-23): +15-20% Q3 / +3-8% Q4 — vs experts +20-25% Q3 (Redburn/Sherman/Edison) and market chatter +30-40%. Direction agreed; magnitude is the debate.
dram-pricing-2026.scope_note: The 06-23 TrendForce +8-13% Q3 is SUPERSEDED — don't cite it as current.
dram-pricing-2026.debate: Magnitude of 2H26 DRAM hikes: TrendForce conservative vs expert +20-25% vs chatter +30-40%.
